rulings.Case Title: JASON ERICKSON VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Bifurcate The Issue Of Product Identification
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 18, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Victaulic Company¿s motion to bifurcate trial to try product identification as it applies to Victaulic Company and request for sanctions is denied without prejudice. The motion may be raised before the trial judge. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

Defendant Continental Automotive Systems, Inc.¿s motion for admission of Martin A. Conn pro hac vice is granted. No opposition filed. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
